item.dc:creator: cowboywest07

item.title: Kettle
item.description: Protests that move quickly are harder to kettle (but are easier to split up as people get left behind). Something important to note is that kettles are used by police to gather intelligence. Police may tell you that you will be allowed to leave if you provide your name and your address, if you give information about other protestors etc. Police can and will lie to you, do not trust them. Never talk to cops. Being trapped in a kettle is stressful. Police may decide to arrest a few of you or all of you. Their goal is to gather information and to demoralize you. They might threaten or harass you. Your goal should be to remain calm and to help the people around you remain calm. Remember if nobody talks, everyone walks.
item.dc:creator: neoswhiterabbitt

item.title: “Call the secret doctor, Marge.”
item.description: EDIT: I used the word "brown" not because I'm racist (I was born in Colombia), but because that kinda thing matters to a racist conservative Christian Republican. Otherwise, I could be referring to some dude named Jeff who snuck in from Toronto. Carry on.

item.dc:creator: BenjyX55

item.title: I did a thing?
item.description: Was a little uneasy with a message in the morning asking “Do you remember me?”. Of course I remembered this kid. I hoped he wouldn’t hurt himself or someone else. Wore some Nazi paraphernalia and was just angry. I didn’t think any of my conversations made a difference but maybe they had? He messaged me to tell me he was sorry for how he acted and that he threw away his Nazi pins in the trash. Told me he was doing okay and he wanted to send me recordings of his band. I’m just happy he realized, fuck nazi punks and that he is doing alright. To the teachers out there struggling. I guess we really can make a difference.
item.dc:creator: ilikepiewaytoomuch

item.title: Neat!
item.description:
Source: https://www.thisiscolossal.com/2020/09/schaerbeek-brussels-mosaics/ I stumbled on this today and LOVE the idea, however due to how long I've spent here with y'all, I know it's only a matter of time until someone requests...
And then I had to make a mosaic of Dickbutt, couldn't resist.
item.dc:creator: huntsnhava40

item.title: Abortion Issue
item.description: Remember a fetus is not a baby. Doesn't have consciousness or feelings. Removing it is like cutting off a piece of hair. I've seen some people arguing about the metaphor in the comments and quite frankly I don't care. I have limited time in a day and the above paragraph should be obvious. I shouldn't have to make this post and the United States shouldn't be this way. The whole consciousness point is self-evident. Do we treat those born without brains the same as those who have consciousness? Do they have the same moral weight as someone who is conscious? To me the answer is simple, no. A fetus that lacks these qualities isn't human to me (morally anyways). I don't know why people seem to think otherwise. https://www.scientificamerican.com/article/when-does-consciousness-arise/#:~:text=Consciousness%20requires%20a%20sophisticated%20network,and%2028th%20week%20of%20gestation. Seems to take 6 months give or take. Anyways, there's more to bodily autonomy and all that but that's all I'm adding to this. You can argue fine details in the comments. This has taken a lot of my time as is.
item.dc:creator: jet100a

item.title: I should be asleep but I give you this wholesome dog dump instead
item.description:
His teeth ich, he can't help it
he'll grow into it
well that's just too cute
cuddles
He was securing his bonus
At least it's not wonderwall
I'd be concerned as well
Life goals right there
A true hero
I'm trying to help my friend pull his little lady out of some serious kidney issues stemming from a poisoning she went through a couple years ago. The fight has been long and hard but we still hope they can get out of them in the end. If you can help them out, please do! https://gofund.me/d8e3dd17
item.dc:creator: thedeadredhead

item.title: Donate to competitive races. Help prevent this!
item.description: I'm a healthcare provider, and this terrifies me. We need to mobilize now! Competitive swing races need money to get their message out and whip up voters. Warnock, for instance. Or Kelley, Demmings or Fetterman. Avoid PACs as you don't know where the money will go for sure. We have to maintain majorities +2 senate seats. And we can. And we MUST!

item.title: I didn’t expect to see results so fast
item.description: I’ve been morbidly obese my entire adult life and now I’m in my 40s and crossed 300 I decided it was time to make a change. I’m on a calorie restrictive diet and already seeing progress.

item.title: Sums it up. This is what “personal responsibility” looks like
item.dc:creator: angelinesg7

item.title: PSA! Spread the word!
item.description: So, as many of you probably already know, drug pricing in the United States is a shit show. We have extremely high drug prices, primarily because drug companies can essentially charge whatever they want. There are no laws to protect the public from price gouging of essential medications. There is also no incentive to lower the costs by insurance companies because they know they can just pass along the cost to their clients because they know they don't have a choice. What can be done about this? Well, thankfully there is at least one solution to help stop these awful abuses. Costplusdrugs.com is a company started by Mark Cuban, a billionaire business owner, that buys drugs wholesale from manufacturers, only adds a 15% markup, a $3 charge to prepare the prescriptions, plus a $5 shipping cost, to provide the essential medications to their customers. This means that for over 100 of the most common, generic medications on the market, they are able to offer a 90+% cost savings compared to the common retail price. Even if you have good health insurance, I would definitely consider using this service anyway. Why? Because even though you have good insurance, and therefore don't have to directly pay extremely high prices for your medication, the cost is still paid by your insurance company. To be able to recoup the cost of paying for these expensive medications, the insurance companies will then raise premiums and deductibles to all of their other clients. By completely bypassing insurance altogether, you give them an incentive to lower their premiums and deductibles. I know everything might not work out the way, in theory, it should, given what I have already said, but it will still show that we are not going to stand for paying insane drug prices anymore when there is no reason to do so. In conclusion, I would recommend that you check out the website and to tell others about it as well, and hopefully, we will be able to shift things in a much better direction :)
